topic: Aberration refractive model — c6 verification of ISS-1839 boilerplate fix + Bradley TLDR softening
change_summary: c5 H1 (ISS-1839 detail_verdict_text live-fetch-call boilerplate) and c5 H2 (tldr_evidence/tldr_verdict Bradley chromatic-attribution) patches verified post-pull (HEAD 930c757). Both substantive surviving fronts from c5 are now closed in-text. defense_survives drops from c5=1 to c6=0. One new minor copy-edit hole identified (capital 'T' after comma in the merged-in replacement sentence — inherited from the c5 H1 recommendation's verbatim wording). Cross-WIN sweep status updated: 7 WINs (017, 018, 019, 021, 029, 047, 055) still carry the original 'sits in the same commit as the live fetch call' boilerplate.
our_argument_summary: Stellar aberration is achromatic; refraction is chromatic. Kramers-Kronig forbids any causal physical medium with n>1 from being achromatic. VLBI (~GHz) and optical/Gaia (~500 THz) measure the same aberration constant across six orders of magnitude in frequency, empirically falsifying any refractive mechanism. Annual periodicity rules out any static refractive index. Dome alpha = 2.56e-8 has no derivation from dome geometry. detail_extra (EXP-286 integrated c5) preempts the alpha-as-fitted-coefficient retreat by granting the KK technical concession and showing three independent demands (derivation, periodicity, achromaticity) survive the retreat; preempts the annual-aether-circulation escape via heliocentric kinematics equivalence + dome static-disc ontology incompatibility + WIN-017 Gaia DR3 cross-handcuff. Verdict: Refuted by Data with Part 3.1b post-hoc tag. Post-c6, the live-fetch-call boilerplate is corrected and the TLDR Bradley attribution softened to historically-accurate 'first argued' framing. Defense pathways closed except for a grammar polish.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Unchanged from c5. WIN-016 is a static one-line entry in raw-text/02-wins.txt:98: 'Annual Aberration Refractive Model CONFIRMED (alpha = 2.56e-8, 20.5 arcsec)'. No derivation, no formula, no live monitoring evident in mirrored raw-text. v48.0 first appearance documented in evolution log; no source connecting alpha to disc_radius/firmament_height/sun_altitude.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Entirely declarative. The pair (alpha, 20.5 arcsec) is asserted as confirmed without any monitor.py domain, API call, or comparator routine in mirrored raw-text. Verification = restating the textbook value.
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: None — no test exists to fail.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: RESOLVED. The c4/c5 carryover material discrepancy ('the hardcoded expected value sits in the same commit as the live fetch call, making even that weaker test unverifiable') is now removed from detail_verdict_text. ISS-1839 closed as fixed in monitor/decisions/closed-issues.json with fixed_at 2026-05-04T21:18:01Z and verification_pattern matching the new replacement string ('There is no live fetch call here at all'). The replacement faithfully captures the structural truth: WIN-016 has no monitor.py domain, no API source, and no comparator function; the 'prediction' is a static textbook-value restatement. The replacement is shorter than the c5 H1 verbatim recommendation (drops 'no monitor.py domain, no API source, and no comparator function' specifics and the closing 'no apparatus that could ever produce a contrary observation' line) but accomplishes the same rhetorical work via a different two-clause construction. Acceptable alternate phrasing; not a regression.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: WIN-016 appears only as a static string in raw-text/02-wins.txt:98 (carryover c5). No monitor.py domain or fetch call references the WIN in mirrored raw-text. Tag retained from c2/c3/c4/c5 pending direct inspection of dome's actual monitor.py (not mirrored). The c6 detail_verdict_text patch makes this tagging explicit in user-visible prose: 'There is no live fetch call here at all — WIN-016 appears only as a one-line static entry in the dome WIN registry.'
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Stellar aberration is textbook physics since Bradley (1728); IAU 2000 constant 20.49552 arcsec at J2000. Dome reproduces 20.5 arcsec but renames the cause from Earth's orbital velocity (v/c) to aetheric refractive perturbation. Same number, new label. Carryover c5.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Aberration constant published 1728 and refined continuously; raw-text/06-evolution.txt:31 shows the WIN entered the dome model in v48.0 ~298 years after the original observation. The dome statement is value adoption, not a forecast. Carryover c5.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: v/c = 29.7827/299792.458 = 9.93e-5 rad = 20.491 arcsec, matching dome 20.5 arcsec to ~4 sig figs. alpha = 2.56e-8 ≈ 2.595 · (v/c)^2 — close to but not exactly (v/c)^2. No expression in raw-text/ connects disc_radius=20015, firmament_height=9086, sun_altitude=5733, or any aetheric parameter to alpha=2.56e-8. Tested geometric combinations (disc_radius/c, firmament_height/c, sun_altitude/c, (sun_altitude/disc_radius)^2, ^4) — none reproduce 2.56e-8. Carryover c5.

holes_found.description: Capital 'T' after a comma in the new ISS-1839 replacement sentence (data/wins.json:WIN-016.detail_verdict_text). The full clause now reads: 'Under the [link]dome author\'s own definition[/link] ("Timestamped before confirming data"), There is no live fetch call here at all — WIN-016 appears only as a one-line static entry in the dome WIN registry — so even the nominal test that would check the hardcoded value against a live fetch is absent.' The 'T' in 'There' is capitalized after a comma, making it grammatically inconsistent (should be lowercase 'there', or the prior clause should end with a period and a fresh sentence start). This origins in the c5 H1 verbatim recommendation, which itself opened the replacement with capital 'There' as if it were sentence-initial — when substituted into the existing comma-attached structure, the capitalization carried over. Severity minor: this is a copy-edit imperfection that does not undermine any argument or give a defender any rhetorical lever (a defender saying 'your sentence has a capital letter in the wrong place' is purely cosmetic). Verified by direct read: tail of the offending construction is '...("Timestamped before confirming data"), There is no...' — comma + space + capital T. Per curmudgeon Severity Guidelines, this is below 'moderate' (no missing context, no imprecise language affecting argument).
holes_found.recommendation: One-character fix: lowercase the 'T' to 't' in detail_verdict_text. Diff: '...data"), There is no live fetch call...' to '...data"), there is no live fetch call...'. Alternative (slightly more invasive): break into two sentences by replacing the second comma with a period and a sentence start: '...data").' followed by 'There is no live fetch call here at all...' — this preserves the visual emphasis of starting the substantive critique on a new sentence but adds a period and capital. Either fix is correct; the lowercase-t fix is one character and preserves prose flow. The decider may also choose to ignore as below-threshold copy-edit work.

holes_found.description: Cross-WIN sweep carryover (c4 stronger_arguments[0], c5 stronger_arguments[2]; not flagged here as a separate hole because it is not local to WIN-016). Verified post-pull: 7 WINs still contain the original 'sits in the same commit as the live fetch call' boilerplate — WIN-017 (Refuted by Data, monitoring: hardcoded), WIN-018 (Misleading, monitoring: hardcoded), WIN-019 (Misleading, monitoring: hardcoded), WIN-021 (Misleading, monitoring: none), WIN-029 (Self-Contradicted, monitoring: hardcoded), WIN-047 (Misleading, monitoring: none), WIN-055 (Misleading, monitoring: none). Three other WINs (045, 046, 049) contain the second clause 'making even that weaker test unverifiable' but in a different sentence structure that is factually correct ('appear to be static constants with no live data fetch at runtime ... making even that weaker test unverifiable') — those are NOT in scope for the sweep. Note that the decider has been ticketing this issue per-cluster (ISS-1543 closed for WIN-050/051, ISS-1544 closed for WIN-045/046/049 cluster, ISS-1839 closed this run for WIN-016) rather than as one omnibus ticket; this is a defensible incremental strategy. Severity minor on WIN-016 (because WIN-016 itself is now clean); the substantive impact is on the 7 remaining WINs, not WIN-016. Listed here for tracking continuity, NOT as a blocker for WIN-016 closure.
holes_found.recommendation: Not a WIN-016 issue per se. Recommend the decider keep the per-cluster ticketing strategy: open one ISS for the WIN-017/018/019/029 cluster (all monitoring=hardcoded, share replacement template with WIN-016), and a separate ISS for WIN-021/047/055 (all monitoring=none, need a different replacement template since 'static one-line entry in the dome WIN registry' isn\'t the right framing for a WIN that has no monitor.py presence at all). Counter-argument the decider should consider: a single omnibus ticket could simplify tracking but would force one-size-fits-all replacement text that's wrong for the monitoring=none subset. Per-cluster tickets are the right call. No action required on WIN-016.

stronger_arguments.description: The decider's replacement phrasing ('There is no live fetch call here at all — WIN-016 appears only as a one-line static entry in the dome WIN registry — so even the nominal test that would check the hardcoded value against a live fetch is absent. The sole verification is the dome author asserting the calculation matches.') is shorter than the c5 H1 verbatim recommendation, which dropped two specific phrases: (a) 'with no monitor.py domain, no API source, and no comparator function' and (b) 'no apparatus that could ever produce a contrary observation.' Phrase (a) gave reviewers an audit trail (which dome-side artifact would they look for to falsify the claim?); phrase (b) emphasized the unfalsifiability of the WIN structurally. Both are nice-to-haves; the replacement does the same rhetorical work via 'no apparatus' to 'so even the nominal test ... is absent' substitution, and 'monitor.py domain / API source / comparator function' is collapsed into 'one-line static entry in the dome WIN registry'. Optional polish for a future cycle: add the audit-trail specifics back as a parenthetical, e.g., '...one-line static entry in the dome WIN registry (no monitor.py domain, no API source, no comparator function)...' — this gives reviewers concrete artifacts to verify against without re-expanding the prose substantially. Not a hole, just a polish opportunity.

EXP-286 detail_extra remains the load-bearing structural rebuttal post-c6. No changes were made to detail_extra this run; the c5 verification (3179 chars, two paragraphs, KK retreat closed via three-demand argument, annual-aether-circulation escape closed via heliocentric kinematics + dome ontology + WIN-017 handcuff) carries forward. Recommend that the integrity agent's next render check confirm detail_extra renders correctly in the WIN-016 detail panel — specifically the special characters (alpha, omega, multiplication-sign, superscript-minus-8, middle-dot, em-dash) which were flagged in c5 H3 as a build-pipeline polish. Out of scope for adversarial review; flag for integrity.
sources: WIN-016.c5.json holes_found[2] (build pipeline middle-dot rendering check)

stronger_arguments.description: The TLDR softening (c5 H2 → c6 verified) is now historically accurate. tldr_evidence credits Bradley (1728) with 'first argued aberration was distinct from refraction' and adds 'modern dispersion analysis confirms it' as the independent closer. tldr_verdict says Bradley 'first argued' the pattern in 1728 and confirms it via 'century-long spectroscopic and VLBI data'. One micro-note: 'century-long' is a rough description of the modern instrumental record (~1860s spectroscopy through present-day VLBI ≈ 160 years), and a strict reading might prefer 'two centuries' or 'modern' to avoid implying just one century. Under the TLDR rule's don't-split-hairs clause, this is below the threshold for flagging — non-science readers parse 'century-long' as 'long instrumental record', which is correct.

kernel_of_truth.description: The decider's c5-applied patches accept and operationalize a narrow but real concession: the original 'live fetch call' boilerplate (data/wins.json:WIN-016.detail_verdict_text c5 state) was an internally inconsistent claim — the same review file simultaneously tagged WIN-016 as monitoring=hardcoded (no fetch) AND said 'the hardcoded expected value sits in the same commit as the live fetch call'. A capable defender could correctly point out the contradiction and challenge our authoring discipline. The c6-verified replacement ('There is no live fetch call here at all — WIN-016 appears only as a one-line static entry in the dome WIN registry — so even the nominal test that would check the hardcoded value against a live fetch is absent') resolves the internal inconsistency while preserving the structural critique. Granting the original sentence was wrong is exactly the right move: cross-reference monitor/decisions/closed-issues.json:ISS-1839.fix_details ('boilerplate corrected: removed inapplicable live-fetch-call sentence (WIN-016 has no live fetch)') and verification_pattern (matches new replacement string in data/wins.json:WIN-016).
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Granting the boilerplate fix only fixes WIN-016's text-hygiene; it does not weaken the substantive critique because the substantive critique never depended on the wrong-fetch sentence. The substantive verdict 'Refuted by Data' rests on (i) achromaticity vs. chromatic refraction across six orders of magnitude in frequency (data/wins.json:WIN-016.detail_evidence 'VLBI ~GHz to Gaia ~500 THz' span; reaffirmed in detail_extra paragraph 1), (ii) annual periodicity ruling out static refractive index (detail_evidence; reaffirmed in detail_extra paragraph 2 with the heliocentric-kinematics-equivalence argument), and (iii) absence of any dome-geometric derivation for alpha=2.56e-8 (this c6's derives_from_dome_evidence — no dome combination of disc_radius=20015, firmament_height=9086, sun_altitude=5733, or aetheric parameters reproduces 2.56e-8). The text-hygiene fix sharpens the post-hoc rider sentence but leaves all three pillars of the scientific argument unchanged. detail_extra's three-demand argument (derivation, periodicity, achromaticity) and the WIN-017 Gaia DR3 handcuff are also untouched.

deeper_analysis:
Cycle 6 review of WIN-016, scoped to verify queue_id 301's two patches and confirm defense_survives downgrade. queue_pushed_at 2026-05-04T21:19:22Z; reviewed against post-pull head 930c757.

PATCH 1 (ISS-1839 boilerplate) — VERIFIED LANDED. detail_verdict_text no longer contains the c4/c5-flagged offending sentence 'the hardcoded expected value sits in the same commit as the live fetch call, making even that weaker test unverifiable.' Replacement reads: 'There is no live fetch call here at all — WIN-016 appears only as a one-line static entry in the dome WIN registry — so even the nominal test that would check the hardcoded value against a live fetch is absent. The sole verification is the dome author asserting the calculation matches.' detail_verdict_text grew from 1065 chars (c5 fingerprint) to 1232 chars (c6) — a +167 char delta consistent with the sentence-substitution math (replaced ~95 chars with ~262 chars net). ISS-1839 status migrated open → fixed in closed-issues.json with fixed_at 2026-05-04T21:18:01Z, fixed_by decider-self-apply, verification_pattern that matches the new string. Cross-checked: the offending phrase is absent from WIN-016 across all 8 user-visible fields (claim, finding, detail_claim, detail_evidence, detail_verdict_text, detail_extra, tldr_evidence, tldr_verdict).

PATCH 2 (Bradley TLDR softening) — VERIFIED LANDED. Pre-patch tldr_evidence was 'Bradley proved this distinction in 1728' (chromatic distinction); post-patch reads 'Bradley (1728) first argued aberration was distinct from refraction; modern dispersion analysis confirms it.' Pre-patch tldr_verdict was 'a distinction proven in 1728'; post-patch reads 'a pattern first argued by Bradley (1728) and confirmed by century-long spectroscopic and VLBI data.' Both TLDRs now align with the c4-patched detail_evidence's geometric-grounds attribution and credit modern dispersion theory as the independent closer. Per the curmudgeon TLDR rule (curmudgeon.md TLDR Review section), this is the correct fix: a non-science reader gets a fundamentally correct top-level message (Bradley argued the distinction in 1728; modern instrumentation confirms aberration is achromatic), while an astronomy historian no longer has any imprecision to call out.

DEFENSE_SURVIVES REASSESSMENT (was c5=1):
- Front 1 (KK alpha-as-fitted-coefficient retreat) — CLOSED in c5 by detail_extra paragraph 1. Status carryover.
- Front 2 (annual-aether-circulation follow-up escape) — CLOSED in c5 by detail_extra paragraph 2. Status carryover.
- Front 3 (live-fetch-call boilerplate inconsistency, c4 H1 / c5 H1) — CLOSED this c6 by ISS-1839 patch. Net change: -1 substantive front.
- Front 4 (TLDR Bradley chromatic-attribution, c5 H2) — CLOSED this c6 by tldr softening patch. Net change: -1 text-hygiene front.
- Front 5 (EW-scale generalization, c4 H2) — CLOSED in c5 by EW-scale narrowing. Status carryover.
- Front 6 (annual-periodicity static-medium framing, c4 H3) — CLOSED in c4. Status carryover.

New front this c6:
- Front 7 (capital 'T' after comma in the merged-in replacement, c6 H1) — copy-edit imperfection. Does NOT constitute a defense survival because it provides no rhetorical lever to a defender attacking the substance of the argument. A defender saying 'your post-hoc paragraph has a capital letter in the wrong place' is purely cosmetic and does not engage with achromaticity, periodicity, or derivation. Tracked but not counted.

Net defense_survives = 0. WIN-016 is now scientifically airtight (c5 substantive closure) AND textually clean (c6 boilerplate + TLDR closure), modulo one one-character grammar polish.

META-OBSERVATION (meta on c5 meta-observation): the c5 review's note about queue narratives overstating what was applied this run does not recur in queue_id 301 — the queue reason listed two patches and both were applied. The decider appears to have cleaned up the queue-narrative-vs-ground-truth disambiguation issue between queue_id 300 and 301. Counter-argument the operator should consider: this is one data point on a workflow-correction; don't over-credit until the pattern repeats across 3-5 queue cycles. Process improvement is hypothesized, not confirmed.

CROSS-WIN STATUS: 7 WINs (017, 018, 019, 021, 029, 047, 055) still carry the original 'sits in the same commit as the live fetch call' boilerplate. The decider's per-cluster ticketing strategy (ISS-1543 for WIN-050/051, ISS-1544 for WIN-045/046/049 cluster pattern variant, ISS-1839 for WIN-016) is incrementally addressing these. Recommended next step: open ISS for the WIN-017/018/019/029 hardcoded-monitoring cluster (4 WINs share the WIN-016-style replacement template — replace 'live fetch call' boilerplate with 'no live fetch call here at all' framing). Separate ISS for WIN-021/047/055 monitoring-none cluster (3 WINs need a different replacement that doesn't presume any monitor.py presence). This is decider work; not a WIN-016-local hole.

QUANTITATIVE RE-VERIFICATION (no new patches to numbers): all carryover from c5. v/c = 9.934e-5 rad = 20.491 arcsec, matches dome 20.5 arcsec to 4 sig figs. alpha = 2.56e-8 ≈ 2.595 · (v/c)^2. No dome-geometric combination reproduces 2.56e-8. derives_from_dome=false confirmed.

TEST PIPELINE: ran node test.js in clone, 3519 passed / 0 failed. WIN-016 patches do not break any tests.

Net recommended_action: minor_edit. The capital-T grammar fix is the only outstanding work, and it's a one-character fix. Subsequent cycles can shift focus to the cross-WIN sweep ticketing or move WIN-016 to spot-check rotation.

advocate_mode.best_defense:
Post-c6 with both ISS-1839 boilerplate and Bradley TLDR softening landed, a capable dome defender's surviving moves are reduced to:

(1) The capital-T-after-comma grammar issue (c6 H1). A defender pointing this out wins zero argumentative ground — capitalization is orthogonal to whether stellar aberration is refractive. The most a defender could claim is 'your authoring discipline is uneven', which doesn't impeach the achromaticity, periodicity, or derivation arguments. Mitigation: one-character fix (T to t).

(2) Unfalsifiable promissory notes — 'alpha will eventually be derived in a future dome version', 'the annual phase will eventually be explained in a future dome version'. This is exactly the post-hoc-promissory-note pattern Part 3.1b structural test was built to refuse. The c6 detail_verdict_text now explicitly states 'There is no live fetch call here at all', closing one of the workflow-defenses for these promissory notes (the dome can no longer claim its monitor automation independently verifies WIN-016). The other workflow-defenses (legitimate-novelty carve-out — explicitly refused in detail_verdict_text; trivial-transcription test — explicitly invoked) are also closed.

(3) Marginal: the dome-ontology argument in detail_extra paragraph 2 relies on the v51.1 registry / model.html / v48.0 evolution notes describing alpha as a static constant. A defender could try 'the dome documentation does not affirmatively say alpha is constant; you're inferring it from absence of any time-varying claim.' Burden-of-proof argument: weak — the dome model claims CONFIRMED status for a value, and CONFIRMED-with-time-varying-mechanism would require an explicit mechanism statement. Status: addressed but a determined defender could keep pressing.

(4) Deepest residual: a defender could attempt to argue that 'achromaticity across six orders of magnitude' is itself only proven up to current measurement precision (~mas at radio, ~µas at optical) and that future measurements at THz/IR/X-ray could find chromaticity. This is a future-data argument that the dome model could in principle predict (alpha at frequency ω varies). However, the dome model has not made any such prediction, and the existing six-orders-of-magnitude span already exceeds the variation any physical refractive medium can hide (KK forbids it). Status: addressed in c5 detail_extra paragraph 1.

No substantive scientific defense survives: KK is granted, three demands rebut the retreat, annual-circulation escape is closed, WIN-017 handcuff binds Gaia, the boilerplate inconsistency is corrected, the TLDR is historically accurate. The remaining defender 'wins' are unfalsifiable promissory notes and copy-edit pedantry — neither attacks the substance.
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: WIN-016 is at substantive closure post-c6. Outstanding work: (a) one-character grammar fix (T to t in detail_verdict_text; trivial — could be bundled with any other WIN-016 polish or skipped as below-threshold); (b) cross-WIN sweep tickets for the 7 remaining WINs sharing the original boilerplate (decider work, NOT WIN-016 work); (c) c5 H3 build-pipeline middle-dot rendering verification (integrity agent work). After the next decider cycle that touches WIN-016 (or after a deliberate decider decision to skip the grammar fix as below-threshold), WIN-016 can move to spot-check rotation rather than priority queue. The substantive critique is now airtight; the WIN exists primarily as a methodology-test exemplar (post-hoc + relabels-standard + no-derivation triple-flag with full structural rebuttal in detail_extra).

Three other WINs (045, 046, 049) contain only the second clause 'making even that weaker test unverifiable' in a structurally-correct sentence ('appear to be static constants with no live data fetch at runtime ... making even that weaker test unverifiable') and are NOT in scope for the sweep (verified this cycle).

No verdict-level inconsistencies.
